san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple channels including cable TV, online streaming, and phone access. Live translations in over 50 languages were available for residents who needed them.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Live translations available in 50+ languages
Meeting accessible to residents without in-person attendance
No public comment period announced for this session

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. Residents who had business to discuss about property zoning or related administrative matters will need to wait for the next hearing, which is now scheduled for April 8, 2026 instead.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions postponed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review funding applications for community development projects that help local residents with housing and support services. The commission reviewed grant money available through a federal program and approved minutes from their previous meeting.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding applications for housing and community projects
Review of previous meeting minutes from February
Community development block grant (CDBG) funding decisions

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed a program to help businesses that may have missed renewing their licenses, promoted a summer campaign encouraging residents to shop locally, and explored ways the city can partner with private companies to sponsor community events and offer incentives for retail shops and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses catch up on licensing
Summer Shop Local campaign - encouraging residents to support Cupertino businesses
Shopping center outreach - connecting with major retail areas in the city
Public-private partnerships - city and businesses co-sponsoring events together
Startup and small business incentives - tax breaks or support for new companies

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to handle several postponed or reconsidered items from previous meetings. The council also addressed weeds in the city by adopting a plan to remove overgrown weeds as part of a county-wide program, and opened discussion on how federal housing grant money should be spent in Santa Clara for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program for 2026
Federal HUD housing grant fund allocation for FY 2026/27
Multiple agenda items postponed or revisited from prior meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to handle several routine items including declaring March as Women's History Month. The council approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city buildings as emergency shelters during disasters, and authorized a 3-year contract to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster shelter agreement with city facilities
Email security software purchase ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report addition to agenda
